risk_patterns.name: Unlimited Liability Exposure
risk_patterns.trigger: No limitation of liability clause present, or liability cap set to 'unlimited'
risk_patterns.description: Without a liability cap, financial exposure is theoretically unlimited, exposing the organisation to catastrophic loss.
risk_patterns.impact: Potential company-ending financial exposure from a single claim.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Search for 'limitation of liability' section. If absent, flag as critical.
risk_patterns.remediation: Add liability cap clause. Market standard: 12 months of fees for direct damages.

risk_patterns.name: Inadequate Insurance Coverage
risk_patterns.trigger: No insurance requirements specified or coverage below industry standard
risk_patterns.description: Counter-party may lack sufficient insurance to back contractual obligations, leaving claims unrecoverable.
risk_patterns.impact: Unable to recover losses even when liability is established.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for insurance clause. Verify minimum coverage amounts against contract value.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require professional indemnity, cyber, and general liability insurance with minimum coverage of 2x contract value.

risk_patterns.name: Hidden Fee Escalation
risk_patterns.trigger: Vague pricing language allowing unilateral fee increases
risk_patterns.description: Ambiguous fee structures enable suppliers to introduce unexpected charges mid-contract.
risk_patterns.impact: Budget overruns of 20-50% above planned expenditure.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Look for 'reasonable', 'may adjust', or 'current pricing' language without caps or notice requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define all fees exhaustively. Cap annual increases to CPI or fixed percentage. Require 90-day written notice.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Common in SaaS contracts where 'usage-based' pricing lacks clear thresholds.

risk_patterns.name: Payment Term Asymmetry
risk_patterns.trigger: Payment terms significantly favour one party, e.g., net-90 for buyer but immediate for supplier
risk_patterns.description: Asymmetric payment terms create cash flow risk and indicate power imbalance.
risk_patterns.impact: Cash flow strain and working capital pressure.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Compare payment obligations of both parties. Flag >60-day terms or prepayment requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Negotiate balanced terms. Standard: net-30 or net-45 with late payment interest at base rate +2%.

risk_patterns.name: Unaddressed Currency Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Cross-border contract with no currency specification or exchange rate mechanism
risk_patterns.description: Currency fluctuations can materially alter the economic value of the contract.
risk_patterns.impact: Margin erosion of 5-15% from adverse exchange rate movements.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if contract specifies payment currency and exchange rate mechanism for multi-currency deals.
risk_patterns.remediation: Specify payment currency explicitly. For long-term contracts, include exchange rate adjustment clause or hedge requirement.

risk_patterns.name: No Price Protection Mechanism
risk_patterns.trigger: Long-term contract (>12 months) without price lock, cap, or benchmarking clause
risk_patterns.description: Without price protection, costs can escalate unchecked over the contract term.
risk_patterns.impact: Cumulative cost increase of 30-100% over multi-year terms.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts >12 months, check for price lock period, cap on increases, or benchmarking rights.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include price lock for initial term, annual cap on increases (CPI or 3-5%), and benchmarking rights after year 2.

risk_patterns.name: No Cost Audit Rights
risk_patterns.trigger: Cost-plus or time-and-materials contract without audit rights on invoiced amounts
risk_patterns.description: Without audit rights, there is no mechanism to verify accuracy of cost-based pricing.
risk_patterns.impact: Systematic overcharging undetected for contract duration.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For any variable pricing model, check for right to audit invoices, timesheets, and cost records.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include annual audit rights with access to supporting records. Require repayment of overcharges plus interest.

risk_patterns.name: Consequential Damages Exclusion Gap
risk_patterns.trigger: Mutual exclusion of consequential damages without carve-outs for key risks
risk_patterns.description: Blanket exclusion of consequential damages may eliminate the most significant remedies in practice.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to recover lost profits, business interruption, or data restoration costs.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if consequential damages exclusion has carve-outs for IP infringement, data breach, and confidentiality breach.
risk_patterns.remediation: Add super-cap carve-outs for data breach, IP infringement, and wilful misconduct. Minimum 2-5x base cap.

risk_patterns.name: No Service Credit Mechanism
risk_patterns.trigger: SLA defined but no financial remedy for SLA failures
risk_patterns.description: SLAs without service credits are aspirational targets with no enforcement mechanism.
risk_patterns.impact: No financial incentive for provider to meet SLA commitments.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if SLA includes percentage-based service credits for each tier of non-compliance.
risk_patterns.remediation: Add tiered service credits: 5% for minor breach, 10% for material breach, 25% for critical failure. Cap at 30% of monthly fees.

risk_patterns.name: Excessive Termination Fees
risk_patterns.trigger: Early termination fees exceeding remaining contract value or calculated on full term
risk_patterns.description: Punitive termination fees lock parties into underperforming contracts.
risk_patterns.impact: Effective vendor lock-in through financial penalty.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Review termination fee calculation. Flag fees exceeding 50% of remaining term value.
risk_patterns.remediation: Cap termination fees at reasonable wind-down costs. Declining scale: 100% year 1, 50% year 2, 25% year 3.

risk_patterns.name: Missing Liquidated Damages
risk_patterns.trigger: Performance-critical contract without pre-agreed damage amounts
risk_patterns.description: Without liquidated damages, recovery requires proving actual loss which is costly and uncertain.
risk_patterns.impact: Lengthy disputes over damage quantification.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For delivery or milestone-based contracts, check for pre-agreed damage rates per day/week of delay.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include liquidated damages at 0.5-2% of contract value per week of delay, capped at 10-20% total.

risk_patterns.name: Missing Gain-Sharing Mechanism
risk_patterns.trigger: Outsourcing or managed services contract without efficiency sharing
risk_patterns.description: Without gain-sharing, provider has no incentive to drive efficiencies that benefit the customer.
risk_patterns.impact: Missed cost reduction opportunities over contract term.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: In outsourcing contracts >3 years, check for gain-sharing or continuous improvement obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include annual efficiency targets with 50/50 sharing of savings above baseline.

risk_patterns.name: No Parent Company Guarantee
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract with subsidiary entity where parent has greater financial strength
risk_patterns.description: Subsidiary may lack financial resources to honour obligations without parent guarantee.
risk_patterns.impact: Counter-party insolvency leaves claims unrecoverable.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if contracting entity is a subsidiary. If so, verify parent guarantee or performance bond.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require parent company guarantee or performance bond for the full contract value.

risk_patterns.name: Stranded Cost Risk on Termination
risk_patterns.trigger: No provision for recovery of investments specific to the contract
risk_patterns.description: Contract-specific investments become unrecoverable if contract terminates early.
risk_patterns.impact: Sunk cost write-off of dedicated resources or infrastructure.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for stranded cost provisions in termination clauses, especially for dedicated infrastructure or staff.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include amortisation schedule for contract-specific investments with recovery on early termination.

risk_patterns.name: No Most-Favoured Customer Protection
risk_patterns.trigger: No mechanism to ensure pricing remains competitive relative to other customers
risk_patterns.description: Without MFC clause, customer may pay above-market rates as vendor offers better deals to others.
risk_patterns.impact: Paying 10-30% above rates offered to comparable customers.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For strategic vendor relationships, check for most-favoured customer or benchmarking rights.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include MFC clause requiring vendor to extend better pricing offered to comparable customers.

risk_patterns.name: No Late Payment Interest
risk_patterns.trigger: No interest provision for late payments
risk_patterns.description: Without late payment interest, there is no financial incentive for timely payment.
risk_patterns.impact: Cash flow disruption from delayed payments.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for late payment interest clause. Verify rate is commercially reasonable.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include interest at base rate +2-4% per annum on overdue amounts, compounding monthly.

risk_patterns.name: Prepayment Without Performance Protection
risk_patterns.trigger: Advance payment required without escrow, bond, or milestone-based release
risk_patterns.description: Advance payments without protection expose the buyer to loss if vendor fails to perform.
risk_patterns.impact: Total loss of prepaid amounts if vendor defaults or becomes insolvent.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if prepayment >25% of contract value has escrow, bond, or milestone-based release protection.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require escrow for large prepayments or milestone-based payment schedule with acceptance criteria.

risk_patterns.name: Tax Liability Ambiguity
risk_patterns.trigger: Unclear allocation of tax obligations between parties
risk_patterns.description: Ambiguous tax provisions can lead to unexpected liabilities, particularly in cross-border transactions.
risk_patterns.impact: Unexpected tax liabilities of 15-25% of contract value.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for clear allocation of VAT, withholding tax, and sales tax obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Specify which party bears each tax obligation. Include gross-up clause for withholding taxes.

risk_patterns.name: No Benchmarking Rights
risk_patterns.trigger: Long-term contract without periodic price comparison mechanism
risk_patterns.description: Without benchmarking, pricing drifts from market over multi-year terms.
risk_patterns.impact: Paying above-market rates by 15-30% after year 3.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts >3 years, check for benchmarking clause with right to adjust pricing.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include annual benchmarking right after year 2 with adjustment mechanism if pricing exceeds market by >10%.

risk_patterns.name: Missing Liability Floor
risk_patterns.trigger: Liability cap set but no minimum floor to prevent trivial cap amounts
risk_patterns.description: Very low liability caps (e.g., tied to last month's fees) provide negligible protection.
risk_patterns.impact: Effective immunity from liability through minimal cap.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if liability cap has a minimum floor amount, especially when tied to variable fees.
risk_patterns.remediation: Set minimum liability floor of 6-12 months fees or a fixed amount, whichever is greater.

risk_patterns.name: No Set-Off Rights
risk_patterns.trigger: No right to set off amounts owed against service credits or damages
risk_patterns.description: Without set-off rights, must pursue separate claims even when amounts are mutually owed.
risk_patterns.impact: Administrative burden and cash flow impact of pursuing separate claims.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for set-off clause allowing deduction of agreed credits from invoices.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include mutual set-off rights for undisputed amounts.

risk_patterns.name: No Open Book Accounting
risk_patterns.trigger: Cost-plus contract without transparency into actual costs
risk_patterns.description: Without open-book requirements, no ability to verify cost-based pricing is accurate.
risk_patterns.impact: Systematic cost inflation of 10-20% undetected.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For cost-plus or time-and-materials contracts, check for open-book accounting obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require monthly open-book reporting with right to audit underlying cost records.

risk_patterns.name: Software Escrow Not Established
risk_patterns.trigger: Software license without source code escrow arrangement
risk_patterns.description: If vendor ceases to trade, customer loses access to critical software without escrow.
risk_patterns.impact: Complete loss of access to business-critical software.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For critical software dependencies, check for source code escrow with release triggers.
risk_patterns.remediation: Establish source code escrow with independent agent. Triggers: insolvency, material breach, cessation of support.

risk_patterns.name: No Invoice Dispute Mechanism
risk_patterns.trigger: No defined process for disputing invoiced amounts
risk_patterns.description: Without a dispute mechanism, parties may withhold payment entirely or face service suspension.
risk_patterns.impact: Service disruption or relationship damage during billing disputes.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for invoice dispute process with timeframes, escalation, and continued service obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include 30-day dispute window. Undisputed amounts must be paid. Disputed amounts subject to good-faith resolution.

risk_patterns.name: Inflexible Volume Commitments
risk_patterns.trigger: Minimum volume or spend commitment without adjustment mechanism
risk_patterns.description: Fixed volume commitments without flexibility clauses create take-or-pay risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Paying for unused capacity or services.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if minimum commitments have adjustment bands (typically +/-20%) and annual rebaseline rights.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include +/-20% volume flexibility band, annual rebaseline, and material change of business carve-out.

risk_patterns.name: Unallocated Transition Costs
risk_patterns.trigger: No allocation of costs for contract transition or migration
risk_patterns.description: Undefined transition costs lead to disputes and delays during contract exit.
risk_patterns.impact: Unexpected costs of 10-25% of annual contract value during transition.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for transition assistance clause with defined cost allocation and pricing.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include transition assistance at prevailing rates for up to 12 months, with incumbent required to provide cooperation.

risk_patterns.name: Uncapped Indemnification Obligations
risk_patterns.trigger: Indemnification obligations not subject to any financial cap
risk_patterns.description: Uncapped indemnities can exceed the entire contract value many times over.
risk_patterns.impact: Unlimited financial exposure through indemnification claims.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if indemnification obligations are subject to the general liability cap or a separate super-cap.
risk_patterns.remediation: Apply super-cap of 2-5x annual fees for indemnification obligations. Require mitigation duties.

risk_patterns.name: No Earn-Back Mechanism for Service Credits
risk_patterns.trigger: Service credits accumulate without any mechanism for provider to demonstrate improvement
risk_patterns.description: Without earn-back, service credits become a one-way financial drain that may incentivise contract termination over improvement.
risk_patterns.impact: Provider may prefer termination over continuous service credit outflow.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if service credit mechanism includes earn-back provisions for sustained performance improvement.
risk_patterns.remediation: Allow 50% earn-back of credits if provider achieves above-target SLA for 3 consecutive months.

risk_patterns.name: No Letter of Credit for High-Value Contract
risk_patterns.trigger: High-value contract (>EUR 1M) with unknown or financially weak counter-party without financial guarantee
risk_patterns.description: Large contracts with unproven counter-parties carry significant credit risk without financial guarantees.
risk_patterns.impact: Total loss of delivered value if counter-party defaults.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts >EUR 1M, assess counter-party credit rating. If below investment grade, check for financial guarantee.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require irrevocable letter of credit or bank guarantee for 3-6 months of contract value.

risk_patterns.name: No Cap on Contractual Penalties
risk_patterns.trigger: Contractual penalties (liquidated damages, service credits) without aggregate cap
risk_patterns.description: Uncapped penalties can accumulate beyond reasonable relationship to actual loss.
risk_patterns.impact: Penalty accumulation exceeding contract value.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if all penalty mechanisms have individual and aggregate caps.
risk_patterns.remediation: Cap individual penalties at 10-20% and aggregate penalties at 30% of affected fees. Include termination right at cap.

risk_patterns.name: No Service Level Agreement
risk_patterns.trigger: Service contract without defined performance metrics or targets
risk_patterns.description: Without SLAs, there are no measurable commitments against which to hold the provider accountable.
risk_patterns.impact: No basis for remedies when service quality deteriorates.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for uptime, response time, and resolution time commitments with measurable targets.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define SLAs: uptime (99.9%), response time (P1: 15min, P2: 1hr), resolution time (P1: 4hr, P2: 8hr).

risk_patterns.name: No Change Control Process
risk_patterns.trigger: No formal process for managing changes to scope, services, or requirements
risk_patterns.description: Without change control, scope creep and unmanaged changes cause disputes and delivery failures.
risk_patterns.impact: Uncontrolled scope expansion and budget overruns.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for change control procedure covering request, impact assessment, approval, and implementation.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include change control procedure: written request, impact assessment within 5 days, mutual written approval required.

risk_patterns.name: No Escalation Procedure
risk_patterns.trigger: No defined escalation path for issues that cannot be resolved at operational level
risk_patterns.description: Without escalation procedures, operational issues stagnate and escalate to legal disputes unnecessarily.
risk_patterns.impact: Delayed issue resolution and relationship deterioration.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for tiered escalation: operational contact, management, executive, formal dispute resolution.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define 3-tier escalation with timeframes: operational (5 days), management (10 days), executive (15 days).

risk_patterns.name: Vendor Lock-In Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: No data portability, proprietary formats, or excessive switching costs embedded in contract
risk_patterns.description: Contract structures that create dependency make it practically impossible to change providers.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to change providers even when performance is poor.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for data portability, standard formats, transition assistance, and reasonable termination fees.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require data export in standard formats, 12-month transition assistance, and declining termination fees.

risk_patterns.name: No Disaster Recovery Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Critical service contract without DR/BCP requirements
risk_patterns.description: Without DR obligations, service may have no recovery capability after major incident.
risk_patterns.impact: Extended or permanent service outage after disaster event.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for RPO/RTO targets, DR testing requirements, and BCP obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define RPO (1hr), RTO (4hr) for critical services. Require annual DR testing with customer notification.

risk_patterns.name: Key Person Dependency
risk_patterns.trigger: Service delivery depends on named individuals without succession planning
risk_patterns.description: Service quality and continuity at risk if key individuals leave or become unavailable.
risk_patterns.impact: Service disruption or quality degradation when key personnel change.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for key person clauses, knowledge transfer requirements, and succession planning.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include key person clause with: 30-day notice of departure, knowledge transfer obligation, and right to approve replacements.

risk_patterns.name: No Performance Metrics
risk_patterns.trigger: Service described qualitatively without measurable KPIs
risk_patterns.description: Without quantifiable metrics, performance assessment is subjective and disputed.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to objectively assess service quality.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for specific, measurable KPIs with baselines, targets, and measurement methodology.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define 5-10 KPIs with: baseline, target, measurement method, reporting frequency, and consequence of miss.

risk_patterns.name: No Reporting Obligations
risk_patterns.trigger: No requirement for provider to report on performance, incidents, or status
risk_patterns.description: Without reporting, customer lacks visibility into service delivery and emerging issues.
risk_patterns.impact: Issues discovered only when they become critical.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for monthly reporting requirements covering performance, incidents, capacity, and risks.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require monthly performance reports, incident logs, and quarterly service review meetings.

risk_patterns.name: No Acceptance Criteria
risk_patterns.trigger: Deliverable-based contract without defined acceptance testing process
risk_patterns.description: Without acceptance criteria, disputes arise over whether deliverables meet requirements.
risk_patterns.impact: Forced acceptance of substandard deliverables or protracted disputes.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for acceptance testing process with defined criteria, test period, and rejection rights.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define acceptance criteria, 30-day testing period, right to reject with specifics, and 2 cure attempts before termination.

risk_patterns.name: No Governance Framework
risk_patterns.trigger: Complex contract without governance structure for ongoing management
risk_patterns.description: Without governance, strategic issues are not addressed and the relationship drifts.
risk_patterns.impact: Misalignment between business needs and service delivery.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts >EUR 500K or >2 years, check for governance schedule with meeting cadence and decision rights.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include governance schedule: monthly operational reviews, quarterly steering committee, annual strategic review.

risk_patterns.name: No Capacity Management
risk_patterns.trigger: Scalable service without capacity planning or burst provisions
risk_patterns.description: Without capacity management, service may fail under peak load or growth.
risk_patterns.impact: Service degradation during peak periods.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for capacity baseline, growth provisions, burst capacity, and scaling commitments.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define capacity baseline with 20% headroom. Include burst provisions and quarterly capacity reviews.

risk_patterns.name: No Incident Management Process
risk_patterns.trigger: No defined process for handling service incidents
risk_patterns.description: Without incident management, response to outages is ad hoc and slow.
risk_patterns.impact: Extended outage duration due to uncoordinated response.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for incident classification, notification requirements, response targets, and root cause analysis.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define P1-P4 classification with notification within 15 minutes for P1. Require RCA within 5 business days.

risk_patterns.name: Single Point of Failure in Service Design
risk_patterns.trigger: Service architecture with no redundancy or failover requirements
risk_patterns.description: Single points of failure create availability risk that SLA targets cannot mask.
risk_patterns.impact: Complete service outage from single component failure.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for redundancy and failover requirements in service description or technical schedule.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require N+1 redundancy for critical components, automatic failover, and geographic distribution.

risk_patterns.name: No Exit Management Plan
risk_patterns.trigger: No structured process for contract exit and transition
risk_patterns.description: Without exit planning, transitions are chaotic, expensive, and risk service disruption.
risk_patterns.impact: 6-12 month transition delays and 2-3x expected costs.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for exit management schedule with timeline, responsibilities, data migration, and knowledge transfer.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include exit management plan as schedule. Require: 12-month notice, transition assistance, data migration support.

risk_patterns.name: Uncontrolled Subcontracting
risk_patterns.trigger: No restrictions or approval rights on subcontracting
risk_patterns.description: Unrestricted subcontracting dilutes accountability and may introduce unvetted parties.
risk_patterns.impact: Quality degradation and security risk from unvetted subcontractors.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for subcontracting restrictions, approval rights, and flow-down of obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require prior written approval for subcontracting. Flow-down all material obligations. Retain primary liability.

risk_patterns.name: No Security Requirements Specified
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract handling sensitive data without defined security obligations
risk_patterns.description: Without security requirements, provider's security posture is unknown and unenforceable.
risk_patterns.impact: Data breach or security incident with no contractual recourse.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for security schedule or requirements referencing ISO 27001, SOC 2, or specific controls.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include security schedule requiring: ISO 27001 certification, annual penetration testing, vulnerability management, and encryption standards.

risk_patterns.name: Inadequate Service Description
risk_patterns.trigger: Vague or incomplete description of services to be provided
risk_patterns.description: Ambiguous service descriptions lead to expectation gaps and disputes.
risk_patterns.impact: Persistent disagreements about scope inclusions and exclusions.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for detailed service description schedule listing all deliverables, inclusions, exclusions, and assumptions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Create comprehensive service description as schedule. List all deliverables with acceptance criteria.

risk_patterns.name: No Business Continuity Testing
risk_patterns.trigger: BCP exists but no requirement for regular testing
risk_patterns.description: Untested business continuity plans frequently fail when activated.
risk_patterns.impact: BCP failure during actual incident.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for annual BCP/DR testing requirements with customer participation rights.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require annual BCP testing with: test plan, customer notification, results reporting, and remediation of findings.

risk_patterns.name: No Knowledge Transfer Obligations
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract termination provisions without knowledge transfer requirements
risk_patterns.description: Without knowledge transfer, institutional knowledge is lost on contract transition.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to effectively transition to replacement provider.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for knowledge transfer obligations in termination and transition assistance clauses.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require documented knowledge transfer over 90-day period including: processes, configurations, incident history, and lessons learned.

risk_patterns.name: No Personnel Background Check Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Provider staff accessing sensitive systems without background check requirements
risk_patterns.description: Without background checks, insider threat risk from provider personnel is unmanaged.
risk_patterns.impact: Insider threat or security incident from unvetted personnel.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for background check requirements for provider personnel with access to sensitive data or systems.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require DBS/CRB equivalent checks for all personnel with access to sensitive data. Annual re-verification.

risk_patterns.name: Missing Data Processing Agreement
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract involves personal data processing without a DPA/DPA addendum
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Article 28 requires a written agreement when a controller engages a processor. Absence is a direct regulatory violation.
risk_patterns.impact: GDPR infringement with fines up to EUR 10M or 2% of global turnover.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: If contract involves any personal data processing, check for DPA as schedule or standalone agreement.
risk_patterns.remediation: Add DPA covering: processing instructions, security measures, sub-processors, breach notification, audit rights, deletion on termination.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Italian DPA fined EUR 20M for processing without adequate contractual safeguards.

risk_patterns.name: No Regulatory Audit Rights
risk_patterns.trigger: DPA or outsourcing contract without right to audit processor/provider
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Article 28(3)(h) requires controllers to have audit rights over processors. DORA mandates audit rights for financial entities.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory non-compliance and inability to demonstrate accountability.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check DPA for audit rights clause. Verify scope covers: premises, systems, records, sub-processors.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include comprehensive audit rights: on-site and remote, with 30-day notice, at controller's expense, including sub-processor coverage.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Breach Notification Clause
risk_patterns.trigger: DPA without breach notification timeframe or content requirements
risk_patterns.description: GDPR requires processor to notify controller 'without undue delay'. Without contractual specifics, notification may be too late.
risk_patterns.impact: Missed 72-hour notification window to supervisory authority.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for breach notification clause with: timeframe (24-48 hours), content requirements, and cooperation obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require breach notification within 24 hours with: nature of breach, categories affected, likely consequences, and mitigation measures.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: British Airways fined GBP 20M partly due to delayed breach notification from processor.

risk_patterns.name: Inadequate Cross-Border Transfer Mechanism
risk_patterns.trigger: Personal data transferred outside EEA without adequate transfer mechanism
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Chapter V requires appropriate safeguards for international data transfers.
risk_patterns.impact: GDPR infringement with fines up to EUR 20M or 4% of global turnover.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if data may be processed outside EEA. If so, verify transfer mechanism: adequacy decision, SCCs, BCRs.
risk_patterns.remediation: Implement SCCs (Module 2 for C2P or Module 3 for P2P), conduct TIA, and implement supplementary measures per Schrems II.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Meta fined EUR 1.2B for US data transfers without adequate safeguards.

risk_patterns.name: No Record-Keeping Obligations
risk_patterns.trigger: Processor contract without obligation to maintain records of processing activities
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Article 30 requires processors to maintain records. Contract should mandate this.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to demonstrate compliance during regulatory audit.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check DPA for obligation to maintain ROPA (Records of Processing Activities).
risk_patterns.remediation: Require processor to maintain ROPA per Article 30(2) and make available on request within 5 business days.

risk_patterns.name: No DPIA Support Obligation
risk_patterns.trigger: DPA without obligation for processor to assist with data protection impact assessments
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Article 28(3)(f) requires processors to assist controllers with DPIAs.
risk_patterns.impact: Incomplete DPIA due to lack of processor cooperation.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check DPA for DPIA assistance obligation and provision of information about processing.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include obligation to provide information needed for DPIA within 15 business days of request.

risk_patterns.name: NIS2 Supply Chain Security Gaps
risk_patterns.trigger: ICT supply chain contract without NIS2-compliant security measures
risk_patterns.description: NIS2 Article 21(2)(d) requires appropriate supply chain security measures.
risk_patterns.impact: NIS2 non-compliance with fines up to EUR 10M or 2% of turnover.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For essential/important entity contracts, check for NIS2 supply chain security requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include: vulnerability management, security incident notification, patching requirements, access control, and security certification obligations.

risk_patterns.name: DORA Non-Compliance in ICT Contract
risk_patterns.trigger: Financial entity ICT outsourcing without DORA-mandated contractual provisions
risk_patterns.description: DORA Article 30 mandates specific contractual provisions for ICT services to financial entities.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory sanctions from financial supervisory authority.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For financial sector ICT contracts, check for all DORA Article 30 required provisions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Add DORA schedule covering: service description, data location, security testing, audit rights, exit strategy, incident reporting, subcontracting approval.

risk_patterns.name: No Sub-Processor Control Mechanism
risk_patterns.trigger: DPA without sub-processor approval mechanism or notification
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Article 28(2) requires prior specific or general authorisation for sub-processors.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of control over data processing chain.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for sub-processor clause: approval mechanism, list of current sub-processors, notification of changes, objection rights.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require: prior general authorisation with notification, 30-day objection period, right to terminate if objection not resolved.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Deletion on Termination
risk_patterns.trigger: DPA without clear data return and deletion obligations on contract end
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Article 28(3)(g) requires deletion or return of personal data after processing ends.
risk_patterns.impact: Continued processing of personal data without legal basis after contract end.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for data return/deletion clause with: timeframe, certification of deletion, and exception for legal holds.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require return or deletion within 30 days of termination, with written certification and destruction of all copies.

risk_patterns.name: Inadequate Security Measures in DPA
risk_patterns.trigger: DPA without specific or measurable security requirements
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Article 32 requires appropriate technical and organisational measures. Vague references are insufficient.
risk_patterns.impact: Unenforceable security obligations and potential data breaches.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for specific TOMs: encryption, access control, backup, testing, incident response.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include security schedule with: encryption standards, access control, vulnerability management, incident response, annual testing, and ISO 27001 certification.

risk_patterns.name: Incomplete GDPR Article 28 Coverage
risk_patterns.trigger: DPA missing one or more mandatory GDPR Article 28(3) provisions
risk_patterns.description: Article 28(3) lists 8 mandatory provisions. Incomplete coverage is regulatory non-compliance.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory finding on DPA adequacy during audit.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check DPA against Article 28(3)(a)-(h) checklist systematically.
risk_patterns.remediation: Ensure DPA covers all 8 requirements: instructions, confidentiality, security, sub-processors, data subject rights, deletion, audit rights, DPIA assistance.

risk_patterns.name: No Documented Processing Instructions
risk_patterns.trigger: DPA without clear description of processing purposes, categories, and scope
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Article 28(3)(a) requires processing only on documented instructions from the controller.
risk_patterns.impact: Processor may process data beyond intended scope.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for processing scope annex defining: subject matter, duration, nature, purpose, types of personal data, categories of data subjects.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include processing scope annex per Article 28(3) covering all required elements.

risk_patterns.name: No Personnel Confidentiality Obligation
risk_patterns.trigger: DPA without requirement for processor staff to be bound by confidentiality
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Article 28(3)(b) requires processor to ensure persons processing data are under confidentiality obligation.
risk_patterns.impact: Staff access to personal data without confidentiality commitment.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check DPA for requirement that all personnel with access are bound by confidentiality.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require all personnel processing personal data to be bound by statutory or contractual confidentiality obligations.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Subject Rights Assistance
risk_patterns.trigger: DPA without obligation to assist with data subject access, deletion, or portability requests
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Article 28(3)(e) requires processor to assist controller with data subject rights obligations.
risk_patterns.impact: Failure to respond to data subject requests within 30-day deadline.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for data subject rights assistance clause with: scope, response time, and technical capability.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require processor to assist with data subject requests within 5 business days, including technical measures for access, deletion, and portability.

risk_patterns.name: No Transfer Impact Assessment
risk_patterns.trigger: SCCs executed without documented Transfer Impact Assessment
risk_patterns.description: Post-Schrems II, TIA is required before relying on SCCs for transfers to non-adequate countries.
risk_patterns.impact: SCCs may be insufficient without supplementary measures identified by TIA.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if TIA has been conducted documenting: laws of destination country, risk assessment, supplementary measures.
risk_patterns.remediation: Conduct TIA per EDPB recommendations. Document findings and implement supplementary measures as needed.

risk_patterns.name: Missing DORA Exit Strategy
risk_patterns.trigger: Financial entity ICT contract without documented exit strategy per DORA Article 28
risk_patterns.description: DORA requires financial entities to have exit strategies for critical ICT providers.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory finding and inability to transition critical services.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For financial sector ICT contracts, check for documented exit strategy with transition plan and timelines.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include exit strategy schedule per DORA Article 28(8) covering: transition planning, data migration, knowledge transfer, and alternative providers.

risk_patterns.name: DORA Incident Reporting Gap
risk_patterns.trigger: Financial entity ICT contract without DORA-compliant incident reporting provisions
risk_patterns.description: DORA Article 19 requires reporting of major ICT incidents. Contract must facilitate this.
risk_patterns.impact: Failure to report major ICT incidents to competent authority within deadlines.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for incident reporting obligations aligned with DORA classification and timelines.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include ICT incident reporting clause: initial notification within 4 hours, intermediate report within 72 hours, final report within 1 month.

risk_patterns.name: NIS2 Incident Notification Gap
risk_patterns.trigger: Essential/important entity contract without NIS2-compliant incident notification
risk_patterns.description: NIS2 Article 23 requires notification of significant incidents. Supply chain contracts must facilitate this.
risk_patterns.impact: Failure to notify CSIRT within 24 hours of significant incident.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For NIS2 in-scope entities, check for incident notification aligned with Article 23 timelines.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require: early warning within 24 hours, incident notification within 72 hours, final report within 1 month.

risk_patterns.name: No Supplementary Measures for SCCs
risk_patterns.trigger: SCCs relied upon for high-risk transfers without supplementary technical measures
risk_patterns.description: EDPB guidance requires supplementary measures when SCCs alone are insufficient.
risk_patterns.impact: Transfer mechanism invalidated by supervisory authority.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For transfers to countries with government surveillance laws, check for supplementary measures: encryption, pseudonymisation, access controls.
risk_patterns.remediation: Implement supplementary measures per EDPB Recommendations 01/2020: encryption in transit and at rest with customer-held keys, pseudonymisation, access restrictions.

risk_patterns.name: No DPO Contact Information
risk_patterns.trigger: DPA without identification of Data Protection Officer or contact for data protection queries
risk_patterns.description: Parties should identify DPO or data protection contact for Article 37-39 compliance.
risk_patterns.impact: Difficulty coordinating on data protection matters.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check DPA for DPO identification or data protection contact details.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include DPO or data protection contact details for both parties in DPA.

risk_patterns.name: No Lawful Basis Specified
risk_patterns.trigger: Processing activities without specified lawful basis under GDPR Article 6
risk_patterns.description: Every processing activity requires a valid lawful basis. Contracts should confirm this.
risk_patterns.impact: Processing without lawful basis is a fundamental GDPR violation.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check processing scope annex for specified lawful basis for each processing activity.
risk_patterns.remediation: Document lawful basis (consent, contract, legitimate interest, etc.) for each processing activity in DPA annex.

risk_patterns.name: DORA Security Testing Requirements Missing
risk_patterns.trigger: Financial entity ICT contract without DORA Article 26-27 security testing provisions
risk_patterns.description: DORA requires digital operational resilience testing of ICT systems.
risk_patterns.impact: Non-compliance with DORA testing requirements.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For financial sector contracts, check for TLPT and security testing obligations per DORA.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include security testing schedule: annual vulnerability assessments, triennial TLPT, cooperation with customer testing programmes.

risk_patterns.name: DORA Subcontracting Controls Missing
risk_patterns.trigger: Financial entity ICT contract without DORA Article 29 subcontracting provisions
risk_patterns.description: DORA requires specific controls on subcontracting for critical or important functions.
risk_patterns.impact: Non-compliance with DORA subcontracting requirements.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for DORA-compliant subcontracting clause: approval rights, monitoring, flow-down of requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include: prior approval for subcontracting critical functions, full monitoring rights, flow-down of all DORA requirements.

risk_patterns.name: DORA Data Location Requirements Unmet
risk_patterns.trigger: Financial entity ICT contract without data location transparency
risk_patterns.description: DORA Article 28(7) requires specification of data processing and storage locations.
risk_patterns.impact: Non-compliance with DORA data location transparency requirements.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check contract for specification of processing and storage locations, including sub-processor locations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Specify all data processing and storage locations. Require 90-day notification of any planned location changes.

risk_patterns.name: No Regulatory Change Mechanism
risk_patterns.trigger: Long-term contract without mechanism to adapt to regulatory changes
risk_patterns.description: Regulations change. Without a mechanism to adapt, contract may become non-compliant.
risk_patterns.impact: Contract terms that violate new regulations.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts >2 years, check for regulatory change clause allowing amendment to maintain compliance.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include clause requiring parties to cooperate in good faith to amend contract to comply with new regulations.

risk_patterns.name: No Termination Right for Regulatory Non-Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: No right to terminate if counter-party fails to maintain regulatory compliance
risk_patterns.description: Continued relationship with non-compliant provider exposes customer to regulatory risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory contagion from non-compliant counter-party.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for termination right triggered by material regulatory non-compliance by either party.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include immediate termination right for material regulatory breach, with transition assistance obligations surviving.

risk_patterns.name: No Regulatory Authority Cooperation Clause
risk_patterns.trigger: No obligation to cooperate with regulatory authority investigations
risk_patterns.description: Without cooperation clause, provider may obstruct regulatory investigations affecting customer.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to cooperate with regulatory investigation.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for obligation to cooperate with regulatory investigations and provide access to information.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include obligation for both parties to cooperate with legitimate regulatory investigations and provide requested information.

risk_patterns.name: No Certification Maintenance Requirement
risk_patterns.trigger: Reliance on certifications (ISO 27001, SOC 2) without ongoing maintenance obligation
risk_patterns.description: Certifications can lapse. Without maintenance obligation, baseline security may degrade.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of certified security baseline without notification.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if security certifications are required to be maintained throughout contract term.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require maintenance of specified certifications, immediate notification of lapse, and termination right if not remedied within 90 days.

risk_patterns.name: No Confidentiality Clause
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract involving sensitive business information without confidentiality obligations
risk_patterns.description: Without confidentiality, sensitive business information may be disclosed to competitors or media.
risk_patterns.impact: Competitive disadvantage and market damage from disclosure.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for mutual confidentiality clause covering: definition, exceptions, permitted disclosures, survival period.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include mutual confidentiality clause with 3-5 year survival period, specific exceptions, and remedies for breach.

risk_patterns.name: No Publicity Restrictions
risk_patterns.trigger: No restriction on counter-party's ability to publicise the relationship
risk_patterns.description: Counter-party may use the relationship for marketing in ways that damage brand.
risk_patterns.impact: Brand association with unwanted messaging or contexts.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for publicity clause restricting press releases, case studies, and marketing use without prior approval.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include clause requiring prior written approval for any publicity referencing the relationship.

risk_patterns.name: No Reference Clause Control
risk_patterns.trigger: No control over use of company as reference or in client lists
risk_patterns.description: Being listed as a client without consent can cause reputational and competitive issues.
risk_patterns.impact: Unwanted public association or competitive intelligence leakage.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for clause restricting use of party name in client lists, marketing materials, and references.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require prior written consent for inclusion in client lists, testimonials, or reference activities.

risk_patterns.name: Brand Misuse Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Broad trademark or logo licence without usage guidelines or approval rights
risk_patterns.description: Uncontrolled use of brand assets can damage brand consistency and reputation.
risk_patterns.impact: Brand dilution and inconsistent market representation.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for trademark licence scope, usage guidelines, and approval requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Limit trademark licence to specific approved uses with brand guidelines compliance and prior approval for new uses.

risk_patterns.name: No Anti-Bribery/Compliance Representation
risk_patterns.trigger: No representation regarding anti-bribery, sanctions, or ethical conduct
risk_patterns.description: Association with corrupt or sanctioned entities creates severe reputational damage.
risk_patterns.impact: Reputational damage and potential criminal liability from association.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for anti-bribery, sanctions compliance, and ethical conduct representations and warranties.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include representations on: UK Bribery Act / FCPA compliance, sanctions screening, modern slavery, environmental standards.

risk_patterns.name: Social Media and Public Statement Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: No restriction on social media posts or public statements about the contract or relationship
risk_patterns.description: Uncontrolled social media commentary can cause rapid reputational damage.
risk_patterns.impact: Viral negative publicity from public statements.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for social media and public statement restrictions covering both parties.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include clause restricting public statements (including social media) about the relationship without mutual agreement.

risk_patterns.name: No Ethical Conduct Standards
risk_patterns.trigger: No requirement for adherence to ethical business conduct standards
risk_patterns.description: Without ethical conduct standards, association with unethical practices creates reputational risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Reputational damage from association with unethical practices.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for code of conduct, ethical standards, or corporate social responsibility requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require adherence to code of conduct covering: anti-corruption, human rights, environmental responsibility, and fair labour.

risk_patterns.name: No Joint Crisis Management Protocol
risk_patterns.trigger: No coordinated response plan for reputational incidents affecting both parties
risk_patterns.description: Uncoordinated responses to joint crises amplify reputational damage.
risk_patterns.impact: Conflicting public statements exacerbating crisis.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For strategic partnerships, check for crisis management protocol with joint communication requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include crisis communication protocol: mutual notification, joint statement agreement, designated spokespersons.

risk_patterns.name: No Non-Disparagement Clause
risk_patterns.trigger: No restriction on making negative public statements about the other party
risk_patterns.description: Without non-disparagement, parties may publicly criticise each other during or after disputes.
risk_patterns.impact: Public disputes damaging both parties' reputations.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for non-disparagement clause surviving termination.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include mutual non-disparagement clause surviving for 2 years post-termination.

risk_patterns.name: Ambiguous IP Ownership
risk_patterns.trigger: No clear assignment or licensing of intellectual property created during contract
risk_patterns.description: Ambiguous IP ownership leads to disputes over rights to use, modify, or commercialise deliverables.
risk_patterns.impact: Litigation over IP ownership and inability to use deliverables freely.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for clear IP ownership clause distinguishing: background IP, foreground IP, and improvements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define background IP (retained by originator), foreground IP (customer-owned or licensed), and improvements (clear allocation).

risk_patterns.name: No Non-Infringement Warranty
risk_patterns.trigger: Deliverables provided without warranty that they do not infringe third-party IP
risk_patterns.description: Without non-infringement warranty, customer bears risk of third-party IP claims.
risk_patterns.impact: Injunction preventing use of deliverables plus damages to third party.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for non-infringement warranty and associated IP indemnification.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include non-infringement warranty with obligation to: defend, replace infringing element, or refund if unable to cure.

risk_patterns.name: Open Source Contamination Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: No restrictions or disclosure requirements for open source components in deliverables
risk_patterns.description: Copyleft open source licenses can require disclosure of proprietary code or restrict commercialisation.
risk_patterns.impact: Forced disclosure of proprietary code or inability to commercialise.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for open source policy: permitted licenses, disclosure of components, no copyleft contamination warranty.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require: open source inventory (SBOM), restriction on copyleft licenses, approval for new open source, indemnification for contamination.

risk_patterns.name: No Source Code Escrow
risk_patterns.trigger: Dependency on proprietary software without source code escrow arrangement
risk_patterns.description: If vendor ceases trading or support, customer cannot maintain or modify critical software.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of ability to maintain business-critical system.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For critical software dependencies, check for source code escrow with release triggers.
risk_patterns.remediation: Establish escrow with independent agent. Triggers: insolvency, cessation of support, material breach uncured for 60 days.

risk_patterns.name: Background IP Capture Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Broad IP assignment clause that may capture pre-existing background IP
risk_patterns.description: Overly broad assignment clauses may inadvertently transfer pre-existing IP to the counter-party.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of core IP assets used across multiple clients.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check IP assignment scope. Verify clear exclusion of background IP with defined schedule.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define and schedule background IP. Explicitly exclude from any assignment. Grant non-exclusive licence only where needed.

risk_patterns.name: Moral Rights Issues
risk_patterns.trigger: Work product creation in jurisdictions with inalienable moral rights
risk_patterns.description: In many EU jurisdictions, moral rights cannot be waived, creating modification restrictions.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to modify or rebrand creative work without author consent.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For creative deliverables in EU jurisdictions, check for moral rights waiver to extent permitted by law.
risk_patterns.remediation: Obtain broadest moral rights waiver permitted by applicable law. In jurisdictions where waiver impossible, obtain consent to modifications.

risk_patterns.name: Trade Secret Leakage
risk_patterns.trigger: Trade secrets shared under contract without adequate protection mechanism
risk_patterns.description: Trade secrets lose protection if not properly identified and protected contractually.
risk_patterns.impact: Permanent loss of trade secret status and competitive advantage.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for trade secret identification, marking requirements, access restrictions, and return/destruction obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Identify trade secrets specifically. Require marking, need-to-know access, secure handling, and return/destruction on termination.

risk_patterns.name: No IP Indemnification
risk_patterns.trigger: No indemnification obligation for IP infringement claims
risk_patterns.description: Without IP indemnification, customer bears full cost of defending IP infringement claims.
risk_patterns.impact: Full exposure to IP litigation costs and damages.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for IP infringement indemnification covering: defence costs, damages, and injunction workaround.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include IP indemnification: defend and hold harmless, with replace/modify/refund options. Carve out from general liability cap.

risk_patterns.name: Unclear License Scope
risk_patterns.trigger: IP licence granted without clear scope, territory, or duration
risk_patterns.description: Ambiguous licence terms create uncertainty about permissible use.
risk_patterns.impact: Inadvertent licence violation or inability to use IP as intended.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check licence for: scope (use, modify, sublicence), territory, duration, exclusivity, and field of use.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define licence explicitly: perpetual/term, worldwide/territory, exclusive/non-exclusive, right to sublicence, modification rights.

risk_patterns.name: IP Improvements Ownership Unaddressed
risk_patterns.trigger: No clause addressing ownership of improvements to background IP
risk_patterns.description: Improvements to existing IP create contested ownership rights.
risk_patterns.impact: Dispute over ownership of valuable improvements.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for improvements clause addressing: definition, ownership, licence-back rights.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define 'improvements' explicitly. Allocate ownership consistently with background IP. Grant licence-back to originator if customer owns improvements.

risk_patterns.name: No Non-Compete for IP-Adjacent Services
risk_patterns.trigger: Development contract without restriction on provider using knowledge for competitors
risk_patterns.description: Provider may use knowledge gained to develop competing products or serve competitors.
risk_patterns.impact: Competitive advantage eroded through provider's work with competitors.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For strategic development contracts, check for non-compete or conflict of interest restrictions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include restricted activity clause: no development of competing products using customer IP or confidential information for 12-24 months.

risk_patterns.name: Joint IP Ownership Trap
risk_patterns.trigger: Joint ownership of IP without clear exploitation rights and obligations
risk_patterns.description: Joint IP ownership creates complex exploitation issues varying by jurisdiction.
risk_patterns.impact: Deadlock on IP exploitation and licensing decisions.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if any IP is jointly owned. If so, verify clear exploitation rights and commercialisation rules.
risk_patterns.remediation: Avoid joint ownership where possible. If necessary, define: exploitation rights, licensing rights, revenue sharing, and dispute resolution for IP decisions.

risk_patterns.name: No Software Bill of Materials Requirement
risk_patterns.trigger: Software deliverable without SBOM requirements
risk_patterns.description: Without SBOM, customer cannot assess open source risk or vulnerability exposure.
risk_patterns.impact: Unknown open source and vulnerability exposure.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For software deliverables, check for SBOM delivery requirement in standard format (SPDX/CycloneDX).
risk_patterns.remediation: Require SBOM in SPDX or CycloneDX format. Update with each release. Include all transitive dependencies.

risk_patterns.name: No Reverse Engineering Restriction
risk_patterns.trigger: Software licence without reverse engineering prohibition
risk_patterns.description: Without restriction, licensee may reverse engineer to extract trade secrets or develop competing products.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of trade secrets through reverse engineering.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for reverse engineering prohibition in software licence terms.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include prohibition on reverse engineering, decompilation, and disassembly except as required by applicable law.

risk_patterns.name: Database Rights Unaddressed
risk_patterns.trigger: Data-intensive project without addressing database rights (sui generis right under EU law)
risk_patterns.description: EU database rights are separate from copyright and often overlooked in IP assignments.
risk_patterns.impact: Disputed database exploitation rights.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For data-intensive deliverables in EU, check if IP assignment covers database rights explicitly.
risk_patterns.remediation: Explicitly include database rights (sui generis) in any IP assignment or licence covering data-intensive deliverables.

risk_patterns.name: No IP Ownership Warranty
risk_patterns.trigger: IP assignment without warranty that assignor has right to assign
risk_patterns.description: Without ownership warranty, assignment may be ineffective if assignor lacks rights.
risk_patterns.impact: IP assignment void due to lack of underlying rights.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for warranty that assignor owns or has right to assign all IP being transferred.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include warranty that: assignor owns all rights, is entitled to assign, no third-party encumbrances, and no litigation.

risk_patterns.name: No Employee IP Assignment Chain
risk_patterns.trigger: Vendor creating IP through employees/contractors without verified assignment chain
risk_patterns.description: IP created by vendor's employees/contractors may not vest in vendor without proper assignment agreements.
risk_patterns.impact: Claimed IP assignment is ineffective due to broken chain of title.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for representation that vendor has IP assignment agreements with all personnel working on deliverables.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require vendor representation and warranty that all personnel have executed IP assignment agreements.

risk_patterns.name: No License-Back for Assigned IP
risk_patterns.trigger: Full IP assignment to customer without licence-back for provider's other clients
risk_patterns.description: Without licence-back, provider cannot reuse general methodologies or tools across clients.
risk_patterns.impact: Provider locked out of using own methodologies.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if IP assignment includes licence-back for provider's general tools, methodologies, and know-how.
risk_patterns.remediation: Grant non-exclusive, perpetual licence-back for general tools and methodologies (excluding customer-specific IP).

risk_patterns.name: No Non-Assertion Covenant
risk_patterns.trigger: IP licence without covenant not to assert IP rights against licensee
risk_patterns.description: Without non-assertion, licensor may assert IP claims despite licence relationship.
risk_patterns.impact: IP litigation despite licence agreement.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for non-assertion covenant covering licensed IP and related patents.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include mutual non-assertion covenant for IP used within scope of the agreement.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Deletion on Contract Termination
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract termination without data return or deletion obligations
risk_patterns.description: Without deletion obligations, provider retains customer data indefinitely after contract ends.
risk_patterns.impact: Continued processing of data without contractual basis.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for data return and deletion clause with: timeframe, format, certification, and exception handling.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require data return in standard format within 30 days. Deletion of all copies within 90 days with written certification.

risk_patterns.name: No Sub-Processor Control
risk_patterns.trigger: Data processing without visibility or control over sub-processors
risk_patterns.description: Sub-processors extend the data processing chain with additional risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Data processed by unknown or unapproved parties.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for: list of sub-processors, prior notification of changes, objection rights, flow-down of obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require: current sub-processor list, 30-day advance notification of changes, right to object, alternative provider if objection sustained.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Localisation Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: No specification of where data is stored and processed
risk_patterns.description: Without data localisation, data may be stored in jurisdictions with inadequate protection.
risk_patterns.impact: Data subject to foreign government access or inadequate legal protection.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for data residency requirements specifying permitted storage and processing locations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Specify data must be stored and processed within EEA. Require notification and consent for any location change.

risk_patterns.name: Cross-Border Transfer Without Safeguards
risk_patterns.trigger: Personal data transferred internationally without appropriate transfer mechanism
risk_patterns.description: Post-Schrems II, international transfers require validated safeguards.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory enforcement action for unlawful data transfers.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Map all data flows. For any transfer outside EEA, verify adequacy decision, SCCs, or BCRs in place.
risk_patterns.remediation: Implement appropriate transfer mechanism: SCCs with TIA and supplementary measures, or rely on adequacy decision.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Meta fined EUR 1.2B in 2023 for US transfers without adequate safeguards.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Breach Notification Process
risk_patterns.trigger: No defined process for notifying data breaches between parties
risk_patterns.description: Without notification process, breach response is delayed, risking regulatory deadlines.
risk_patterns.impact: Missed 72-hour notification deadline to supervisory authority.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for breach notification clause with: timeframe, content requirements, format, and cooperation obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require notification within 24 hours with: nature of breach, data affected, likely consequences, mitigation measures, and designated contacts.

risk_patterns.name: Inadequate Technical Security Measures
risk_patterns.trigger: Data processing contract without specific technical security requirements
risk_patterns.description: Vague security commitments are unenforceable and may not meet GDPR Article 32 standards.
risk_patterns.impact: Data breach due to inadequate security controls.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for specific technical measures: encryption standards (AES-256), access control, MFA, vulnerability management, logging.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include detailed security schedule: encryption (AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.2+ in transit), MFA, RBAC, vulnerability scanning, SIEM, annual pen testing.

risk_patterns.name: No Privacy by Design Obligation
risk_patterns.trigger: Development contract without privacy by design requirements
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Article 25 requires data protection by design and by default.
risk_patterns.impact: System design that requires costly retrofitting for compliance.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For development contracts, check for privacy by design requirements in specifications.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include privacy by design requirements: data minimisation, purpose limitation, access controls by default, and DPIA triggers.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Retention Policy
risk_patterns.trigger: No defined retention periods for personal data
risk_patterns.description: GDPR storage limitation principle requires defined retention periods.
risk_patterns.impact: Indefinite data retention violating storage limitation principle.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for data retention schedule with: data categories, retention periods, legal basis, and destruction process.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define retention schedule for all personal data categories. Implement automated deletion at end of retention period.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Classification
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract handling data without classification of sensitivity levels
risk_patterns.description: Without classification, all data receives same level of protection regardless of sensitivity.
risk_patterns.impact: Sensitive data receiving inadequate protection.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for data classification scheme and mapping to protection levels.
risk_patterns.remediation: Classify data: public, internal, confidential, restricted. Map each classification to specific security controls.

risk_patterns.name: No Anonymisation Standards
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract referencing anonymised data without defining anonymisation standards
risk_patterns.description: Inadequate anonymisation may be re-identifiable, keeping data within GDPR scope.
risk_patterns.impact: Data assumed anonymous but actually personal data under GDPR.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: If contract references anonymisation, check for defined standards and verification methodology.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define anonymisation standards per WP29 Opinion 05/2014. Require re-identification risk assessment and testing.

risk_patterns.name: No Encryption at Rest
risk_patterns.trigger: Data storage without encryption at rest requirements
risk_patterns.description: Unencrypted data at rest is vulnerable to unauthorised access from storage compromise.
risk_patterns.impact: Data exposure from storage media theft or unauthorised access.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for encryption at rest requirements with: algorithm (AES-256), key management, and rotation policy.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require AES-256 encryption at rest with: customer-managed keys where possible, annual key rotation, and secure key storage.

risk_patterns.name: No Access Control Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: No specified access control requirements for data processing
risk_patterns.description: Without access control requirements, data may be accessible to unauthorised personnel.
risk_patterns.impact: Unauthorised access to personal or sensitive data.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for access control requirements: RBAC, least privilege, MFA, access logging, and regular review.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require RBAC with least privilege, MFA for all access, access logging, and quarterly access reviews.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Backup Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: No backup frequency, testing, or recovery requirements specified
risk_patterns.description: Without backup requirements, data availability and integrity are at risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Data loss from system failure without recovery capability.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for backup requirements: frequency, retention, testing, offsite storage, and encryption.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require daily backups, 90-day retention, quarterly restore testing, geo-redundant storage, and encryption matching primary data.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Portability Provisions
risk_patterns.trigger: No mechanism for extracting data in portable format
risk_patterns.description: Without portability, data is trapped with provider, creating lock-in.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to migrate data to alternative provider.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for data export capability in standard, machine-readable formats.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require data export in standard formats (CSV, JSON, XML) via API or bulk export. Include in transition assistance.

risk_patterns.name: No Joint Incident Response Plan
risk_patterns.trigger: No coordinated incident response between controller and processor
risk_patterns.description: Without joint response plan, breach management is uncoordinated and slow.
risk_patterns.impact: Delayed and ineffective breach response.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for incident response plan covering: roles, communication, forensics, remediation, and regulatory notification.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include incident response schedule: contact matrix, classification criteria, response procedures, forensics cooperation, and communication plan.

risk_patterns.name: No Penetration Testing Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: No requirement for regular security testing of systems processing personal data
risk_patterns.description: Without pen testing, vulnerabilities may go undetected until exploited.
risk_patterns.impact: Undetected vulnerabilities leading to data breach.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for annual penetration testing requirements with scope including all systems processing personal data.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require annual penetration testing by qualified third party. Share results within 30 days. Remediate critical findings within 30 days.

risk_patterns.name: No Vulnerability Management Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: No vulnerability scanning or patch management requirements
risk_patterns.description: Without vulnerability management, known vulnerabilities persist and create breach risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Data breach through exploitation of known vulnerability.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for vulnerability management requirements: scanning frequency, patch SLAs, and reporting.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require weekly vulnerability scanning, critical patches within 48 hours, high within 14 days, and monthly vulnerability reports.

risk_patterns.name: Shared Tenancy Data Isolation Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Multi-tenant service without data isolation guarantees
risk_patterns.description: In shared environments, data isolation failures can expose one customer's data to another.
risk_patterns.impact: Cross-tenant data leakage.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For multi-tenant SaaS, check for data isolation commitments: logical/physical separation, encryption per tenant.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require logical data separation at minimum, per-tenant encryption keys, and annual isolation testing.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Minimisation Commitment
risk_patterns.trigger: Processing scope broader than necessary for stated purposes
risk_patterns.description: GDPR data minimisation principle requires processing only data strictly necessary.
risk_patterns.impact: Processing excessive data increasing breach impact and regulatory risk.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if data categories collected are necessary for stated processing purposes.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define minimum data set for each processing purpose. Commit to not processing data beyond stated purpose.

risk_patterns.name: No Security Logging Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: No requirements for security event logging and monitoring
risk_patterns.description: Without logging, security incidents cannot be detected, investigated, or evidenced.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to detect breaches or demonstrate compliance.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for security logging requirements: event types, retention period, monitoring, and access to logs.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require: authentication, authorisation, and data access logging. 12-month retention. Real-time monitoring. Customer access to logs on request.

risk_patterns.name: No Secure Development Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Software development without secure development lifecycle requirements
risk_patterns.description: Without SDLC requirements, software may contain vulnerabilities from insecure development practices.
risk_patterns.impact: Vulnerabilities in delivered software due to insecure development.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For development contracts, check for SSDLC requirements: code review, SAST/DAST, dependency scanning.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require OWASP SAMM or similar SSDLC: code review, static analysis, dynamic testing, dependency scanning, and security training.

risk_patterns.name: No Termination Assistance
risk_patterns.trigger: No obligation for incumbent to assist with transition to replacement provider
risk_patterns.description: Without transition assistance, contract exit is chaotic and may cause service disruption.
risk_patterns.impact: Extended service disruption during transition.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for termination assistance clause with: duration, scope, pricing, and cooperation obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include 6-12 month transition assistance at prevailing rates: knowledge transfer, data migration, parallel running, and handover.

risk_patterns.name: No Step-In Rights
risk_patterns.trigger: Critical service without right for customer to assume control in emergency
